description: Behavior Trees, AI Controllers, EQS, navigation, perception, and AI decision-making in UE5

entries.title: Behavior Tree Not Running / AI Does Nothing
entries.description: An AI-controlled character has a Behavior Tree assigned but the tree never executes. The AI stands idle.
entries.rootCause: The Behavior Tree must be started explicitly via RunBehaviorTree() in the AI Controller, or set as the default BT in the AI Controller's properties. The Pawn must also have the correct AI Controller class assigned.
solution: In your AI Controller, call RunBehaviorTree(BehaviorTreeAsset) in BeginPlay or OnPossess
Verify the Pawn's 'AI Controller Class' is set to your custom AIController (not the default)
Check 'Auto Possess AI' on the Pawn is set to 'Placed in World' or 'Spawned'
Ensure the Behavior Tree asset is valid and compiles without errors
Verify the Blackboard asset is assigned to the Behavior Tree
Use the Behavior Tree debugger (F5 during PIE, select the AI) to see the execution state

entries.title: AI Navigation Fails / MoveTo Returns No Path
entries.description: The AI controller's MoveTo or Behavior Tree's MoveTo task fails with 'No Path' or the AI doesn't move to the target location.
entries.rootCause: No NavMesh covers the area. The NavMesh Bounds Volume doesn't encompass the play area, or the navmesh is not rebuilt after level changes. Obstacles may also block the path completely.
solution: Add a NavMesh Bounds Volume that covers your entire playable area
Press 'P' in the editor viewport to visualize the navmesh (green = walkable)
After adding/moving geometry, rebuild navigation: Build > Build Paths
Check agent size: Navigation System > Supported Agents > Agent Radius/Height must fit through doorways
Use RecastNavMesh settings to tune cell size and agent parameters
For dynamic obstacles, ensure they have a Nav Modifier Component or Dynamic Obstacle flag
Enable 'Allow Partial Paths' if the AI should get as close as possible when full path is impossible

entries.title: Blackboard Value Not Being Set / Read Correctly
entries.description: Behavior Tree tasks or services can't read/write Blackboard values. Keys appear null or have wrong values.
entries.rootCause: The Blackboard key name is misspelled, the key type doesn't match what the task expects (Object vs Actor vs Vector), the key isn't defined in the Blackboard asset used by this BT, or the Blackboard Component reference is invalid.
solution: Ensure the Behavior Tree references the correct Blackboard asset
Check key names match exactly (case-sensitive) between BT tasks and the Blackboard asset
Key type must match: 'Object' for UObjects, 'Class' filter must include the expected class
For Actor keys, the Blackboard key must be of type 'Object' with base class set to AActor
In C++ tasks, use GetBlackboardComponent() and verify it's not null
Use the Blackboard debugger during PIE to see current values

entries.title: AI Perception Not Detecting Targets
entries.description: AI Perception component doesn't detect actors. Sight, hearing, or damage senses never trigger.
entries.rootCause: The AI Perception Component is missing or misconfigured on the AI Controller. The target actor doesn't have an AI Perception Stimuli Source component. Sense parameters (sight radius, peripheral vision angle, hearing range) may be too restrictive.
solution: Add UAIPerceptionComponent to your AI Controller (not the Pawn)
Configure at least one sense (Sight, Hearing, Damage, etc.) on the Perception Component
Add UAIPerceptionStimuliSourceComponent to actors that should be detectable
Register the correct stimuli on the source: RegisterForSense (e.g., UAISense_Sight)
For Sight: check SightRadius, LoseSightRadius, PeripheralVisionAngleDegrees
Bind to OnTargetPerceptionUpdated delegate to react to perception changes
Use 'AI Debug' (apostrophe key ') during PIE to visualize perception

entries.title: EQS Query Returns No Results
entries.description: An Environment Query System (EQS) query returns zero results, causing the Behavior Tree task that uses it to fail.
entries.rootCause: The query generator produces items outside the navmesh, the test filters are too strict (all items score 0 or are filtered out), or the query context (Querier, Target) is not set correctly on the Blackboard.
solution: Open the EQS query in the EQS Testing Pawn to visualize results in the viewport
Check that the generator creates items on the navmesh (use 'Project to Navigation' option)
Review test scores - if all items score 0, the test conditions are too strict. Relax thresholds
Ensure query contexts resolve correctly: the Querier's Blackboard must have the target key set
Use 'Filter Only' mode on tests to see which test eliminates all items
Common issue: Distance test filtering out all items because no item is within the acceptable range

entries.title: Behavior Tree Task Never Finishes / AI Gets Stuck
entries.description: A custom Behavior Tree task starts executing but never returns Success or Failure, blocking the entire tree from proceeding.
entries.rootCause: Custom BT tasks that return InProgress must eventually call FinishLatentTask(). If the completion condition is never met (target destroyed, path invalidated, etc.), the task runs forever. Also, TickTask may not be enabled.
solution: Ensure your task calls FinishLatentTask(OwnerComp, EBTNodeResult::Succeeded) or Failed when done
Add timeout logic: if the task runs for too long, fail it gracefully
If using TickTask, set bNotifyTick = true in the constructor
Handle edge cases: what if the target actor is destroyed mid-task? Check and abort
Use Abort handlers (OnTaskAbort) to clean up when the tree forces a branch change
For MoveTo tasks, bind to the OnMoveCompleted delegate

entries.title: AI Crowd Avoidance / Agents Overlapping
entries.description: Multiple AI characters walk through each other or stack on the same position instead of avoiding each other.
entries.rootCause: Crowd avoidance (RVO) is not enabled or configured, or the character movement component's avoidance settings are too weak. The detour crowd manager needs to be active.
solution: Enable RVO Avoidance on the Character Movement Component: bUseRVOAvoidance = true
Set AvoidanceWeight and AvoidanceConsiderationRadius to appropriate values
Use the UCrowdManager for large groups: set 'Use Crowd Manager' in Navigation System settings
Set different avoidance groups for different AI types so they know what to avoid
Increase avoidance radius if agents still overlap
For formation-based movement, implement custom steering instead of relying on RVO alone

entries.title: StateTree Not Transitioning Between States
entries.description: UE5's StateTree (alternative to Behavior Trees) doesn't transition between states. The AI stays in one state even when conditions should trigger a change.
entries.rootCause: Transition conditions are evaluated in order and the first matching transition wins. If conditions are never met (wrong Blackboard values, wrong comparison, event not firing), no transition occurs.
solution: Check transition conditions in the StateTree editor - hover over conditions to see their evaluation
Ensure the data (Blackboard, context) that conditions check is actually being updated
Transition priority matters: the first matching transition executes. Reorder if needed
For event-based transitions, ensure the event is actually being sent via SendStateTreeEvent
Use the StateTree debugger during PIE to see current state and condition evaluation
Add a fallback transition with no conditions as a safety net

entries.title: AI Stuck on NavMesh Edge / Can't Reach Location
entries.description: AI characters get stuck on navmesh edges, corners, or at gaps between navmesh polygons. They jitter or stop moving at certain locations.
entries.rootCause: The navmesh has gaps from geometry that doesn't perfectly align, the agent radius is too large to fit through narrow passages, or the navmesh resolution is too coarse for the environment detail.
solution: Visualize navmesh with 'P' key and look for gaps or missing coverage
Reduce Agent Radius in RecastNavMesh if agents get stuck in narrow areas
Add Nav Modifier Volumes to manually mark areas as walkable/not walkable
Increase Cell Size and Cell Height resolution for more accurate navmesh
Use Nav Link Proxies for jumps, drops, or areas requiring special movement
Set 'bAllowPartialPaths = true' in MoveTo requests so agents get as close as possible
